P3 Wave-2a builder, reporting to Rob. Repo /Users/amanigga/Desktop/TAMAZIA-REBUILD/tamazia-audit-engine, branch p3-evidence @ 7b00e2b. READ FIRST: docs/P3-ACCEPTANCE.md (waves overview), CONSTITUTION.md Rules 2/3/10/11/14, caution.md C-035/C-036/C-046..C-070 range (applicability+breach sections), catalogue/README.md, one full pack (catalogue/packs/uk-legal.json) + catalogue/dist/catalogue.v1.json structure, evidence/crawler/coverage-contract.js (the covered|screened input you consume), facts/README.md (EvidenceBundle). Port source (READ-ONLY): /Users/amanigga/Desktop/TAMAZIA-REBUILD/cowork-os-fresh/src/skills/S008-personalisation-engine/scanners/compliance.js (the old detection scanners - study HOW it detected, port the good detection ideas, not its false-positive classes).

YOU OWN ONLY: breach/proposers/** and fixtures eval/calibration-known-bad/fixtures/p3-proposer-*.{js,json}. Do NOT edit catalogue/packs/** (QA-stamped - NEVER touch), GAPS.md, caution.md, other waves' dirs. Do not git commit.

BUILD the proposer layer - the ONLY producer of breach CANDIDATES (Rule: proposers propose, verifiers verify, adjudicator rules; a proposer NEVER emits a finding, only a candidate):
1. breach/proposers/detection-spec.js - the detection-spec migration: compile catalogue records' prose website_obligations[].elements[] + evidence_type into machine-checkable DetectionSpecs at runtime (derived IN MEMORY from the compiled catalogue - packs stay untouched). Spec shape: {record_id, duty_idx, evidence_type: presence|absence|behavioural|register, surface: visible_text|raw_html|footer|register_row|browser_lane, patterns: [{kind: 'anchored-regex'|'token-set'|'url-path', value, negation_guarded}], page_class}. Anchored per C-009/C-019 (delimiters, no bare prefixes), negation-guarded (port corpus-index.js NEGATION_RX idea), C-035 (detection surface = evidence surface, declared per rule), C-036 (triggers on visible prose only; mechanism-presence may use raw - per rule declaration).
2. breach/proposers/propose.js - propose(bundle, catalogue, coverage) -> candidates[]: for each APPLICABLE record (consume coverage: a rule whose page_class is 'screened' NEVER proposes - C-029; absence rules additionally demand the coverage interlock: min pages + Tier-1 fetched + corpus not truncated, else candidate suppressed with reason - C-024/C-026/C-037), evaluate its DetectionSpec against the bundle. presence-breach: pattern found where prohibited (quote captured verbatim with page url). absence-breach: pattern absent across the DECLARED surface with coverage proven. behavioural: consume bundle.browser.observed (W1c shapes: cookie_pre_consent etc.). register: consume bundle.registers rows. Candidate shape: {record_id, duty_idx, kind, artifact:{type: quote|network_event|register_row|coverage_proof, ...verbatim data...}, page_url, confidence_hint, suppressed_reason?}.
3. Every candidate MUST carry its artifact (Rule 3) - a quote candidate carries the exact verbatim string + url; tests string-match them back to the fixture corpus.
Tests: node:test per module, fixture bundles (synthetic, FAKE_ACT_2099-style law ids only - no real law text outside catalogue, C-071 doctrine). Calibration fixtures: p3-proposer-screened-never-proposes.json (screened rule must not yield a candidate), p3-proposer-unanchored-pattern.js (a DetectionSpec with an unanchored/bare pattern must be REJECTED by spec validation). EXIT: npm test green, lint green, sweep GREEN zero leads in your files, --strict calibration green, health-gate zero on your files. British English, no em dashes, no new deps, no secrets. REPORT to Rob: files+lines, the DetectionSpec grammar with examples, how each evidence_type routes, suppression rules implemented, fixture list, exit codes, honest gaps.

- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- artifact-capabilities: Runtime capabilities a published Artifact can declare — calling the user's claude.ai connectors (MCP) from the page, and future abilities. Load this BEFORE passing `capabilities` to the Artifact tool or writing any `window.claude.mcp` code.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.

1	# CONSTITUTION: tamazia-audit-engine
2	
3	These are the mechanically enforced rules of this repository. Every rule exists because the old estate broke it and shipped a false legal claim, a phantom mint, or a confident zero. A rule with no enforcing gate is theatre, so every rule below names the CI gate or tool that enforces it. If a gate cannot fire, the rule does not exist: fix the gate before writing code that depends on the rule.
4	
5	Enforcement-file status: this repo is at P0, so some named enforcing files are the committed names later phases must land, not files in the tree today. The Part III gate map marks every file **live** or **planned (Pn)**; a prose "Enforced by" reference to a file not yet in the tree means the planned file in that map.
6	
7	This repository is PUBLIC. No secret, token, key, password or connection string may ever appear in any file. This document is not legal advice and the engine's output is not legal advice; a standing not-legal-advice line ships on every rendered audit.
8	
9	Sources: docs/PRD.md §3, docs/discovery/digest-research-llm-agents.md, docs/discovery/digest-research-compliance.md, docs/discovery/digest-claude-ledger-forensic.md, docs/discovery/digest-findings-bible.md.
10	
11	---
12	
13	## Part I: The constitutional rules
14	
15	### Rule 1: One door per fact
16	Every client-facing fact (legal name, jurisdiction, sector, regulator, fine, law name, host, element checklist) has exactly ONE producer module. Consumers read the fact; they never re-derive it. The renderer introduces no facts.
17	
18	**Why:** the old estate had 10 producers of jurisdiction, 8 of host, 6 of sector, 5 of regulator; the stale door is the one the client sees. This class shipped a P0 three times: the ghost jurisdiction, "Sector regulator" printed on 51% of the catalogue, and the £17.5M fine fixed in the DB that never reached the client because three code files kept their own copy (digest-findings-bible §B1).
19	
20	**Enforced by:** `tools/one-door/check.js` (blocking gate, ported from cowork-os PR #342) run inside the sweep (`.github/workflows/sweep.yml` and `tools/sweep/run.js`, wired into `.github/workflows/ci.yml`) on every PR; `tools/fact-lineage/check.js` asserts a single producer per fact and that the renderer imports facts only through the payload contract (activates fully when `payload/schema/facts-lineage.json` lands in P4).
21	
22	### Rule 2: Catalogue-only law facts
23	The compiled catalogue artifact is the ONLY source of law names, citations, fines, penalty bands, regulators and enforcement intel. A literal law name, fine amount or regulator string in engine or renderer code fails CI.
24	
25	**Why:** hand-maintained copies drifted for months: `FW_NAME_CAT` was a frozen 285-code snapshot rendering "Uae Newcode"; `fineRate` regexes applied wrong ceilings to non-fining bodies; the self-learning loop invented statutes with fake legislation.gov.uk URLs (digest-claude-ledger-forensic b64, b65, b97).
26	
27	**Enforced by:** `catalogue/lint/no-literal-law-facts.js` (blocking, scans engine + renderer source for law-name/fine/regulator literals outside `catalogue/`) in `.github/workflows/catalogue-lint.yml`; the render truth-pack in `render-proof/truth-pack.spec.js` rejects any rendered fine, regulator or law title not present in the compiled catalogue.
28	
29	### Rule 3: No artifact, no breach
30	A breach finding must carry a deterministic artifact: a verbatim quote string-matched to the crawled corpus, a captured network event, a register row, or a failing DOM node. No artifact means the finding cannot exist, whatever a regex or a model said.
31	
32	**Why:** every breach Tamazia ever sent before v23.0 was an unreviewed regex match no model or artifact ever verified; a bare-word regex over raw HTML accused a real criminal-defence law firm of being hacked ("sex discrimination", `<slot>`) (digest-claude-ledger-forensic b84, e-adjudicator). Pentest doctrine: "a finding with no reproduction steps cannot be trusted" (digest-research-compliance §D).
33	
34	**Enforced by:** `breach/verifiers/` is the only path from proposer to adjudicator, and the payload schema in `payload/schema/` makes the artifact fields mandatory (missing artifact = schema-invalid payload, mint refused); `render-proof/truth-pack.spec.js` re-matches every rendered quote against the stored corpus word by word in `.github/workflows/render-truth.yml`.
35	
36	### Rule 4: Every gate fails closed and is calibrated against known-bad input
37	A gate that errors, times out or receives malformed input must BLOCK, not pass. Every analyser and gate must demonstrably fail on a seeded bad fixture on every run; a gate that has never fired is assumed broken.
38	
39	**Why:** mandatory audit gates ran AFTER the R2 payload was persisted; `llmPreflight()` failed open; the jurisdiction stage was marked `ran` unconditionally; three evidence layers threw ReferenceErrors on their first line for two whole versions while a fail-open catch shipped every audit as if the model had read the breaches (digest-findings-bible F-0001; digest-claude-ledger-forensic b106).
40	
41	**Enforced by:** `eval/calibration-known-bad/run.js` in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` feeds every gate a corpus of seeded bad inputs each run and fails CI if any gate passes one; `tools/swallow-gate/check.js` (AST-level, repo-wide, also in `.github/workflows/ci.yml`) blocks any catch block that swallows without recording a typed failure state.
42	
43	### Rule 5: Reachable or DORMANT
44	Every module is either provably reachable from the mint entry point or explicitly declared in `DORMANT.md` with a reason. Dead-but-green code is a lie waiting to ship.
45	
46	**Why:** 13 modules / 691 lines of correct, tested, cited legal logic were unreachable from the mint, including `citation-gate.js`, the gate built to make an unevidenced monetary claim impossible, which had never executed once; `statute-rag.js` was required for months and never called (digest-findings-bible §B6).
47	
48	**Enforced by:** madge + dependency-cruiser reachability walk in `.github/workflows/reachability.yml`, blocking: any module not reachable from `mint/` and not listed in `DORMANT.md` fails the PR; `tools/domain-gates/reachability.js` additionally asserts every ACTIVE catalogue rule routes to a scanner branch that can actually fire.
49	
50	### Rule 6: Below-confidence output is quarantined, never shipped
51	Anything the engine cannot assert with evidence at or above the confidence floor routes to quarantine (`needs-review` or `compliance_unassessed`), never into a shipped claim. Ambiguity defaults to withholding the accusation.
52	
53	**Why:** ambiguous LLM entailment verdicts ("Unclear, leaning no") matched neither branch and the finding stayed CONFIRMED with its fine; a foreign-language site got a fabricated 16-breach GDPR cascade because English regexes ran on a French corpus; unreadable bot-walled sites were asserted against (digest-claude-ledger-forensic b135, b1, b126).
54	
55	**Enforced by:** the adjudicator contract in `breach/adjudicator/` (verdict enum has no "maybe ships" branch: only violation / needs-review / pass, unparseable verdict = needs-review); `eval/calibration-known-bad/run.js` includes ambiguous-verdict and non-English fixtures that must land in quarantine; the payload schema rejects findings whose confidence fields are below floor but whose state is `violation`.
56	
57	### Rule 7: A mint is done only when row + HTTP 200 + truth-pack all pass
58	"Minted" means: the DB row exists, the live URL answers 200, and the Playwright truth-pass proves the rendered words match the payload. Anything less is not done, whatever the queue says.
59	
60	**Why:** the old queue reported "done" on 1,004 phantom audits; `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` adopted stale rows while the queue said done; a transport failure masqueraded as an idempotent conflict and a row that was never written was hunted for days (digest-claude-ledger-forensic b102, b103; PRD §1).
61	
62	**Enforced by:** `mint/post-write-assertions.js` runs inside every mint worker (row read-back + live 200 + truth-pack invocation before status flips to done); `tools/mint-reconciler/reconcile.js` in `.github/workflows/mint-reconcile.yml` (scheduled) diffs queue vs audit_pages vs leads and alarms on any done-without-page.
63	
64	### Rule 8: Budgets are caps, never floors
65	Every page, token, retry and time budget is an upper bound. No `Math.max` floors, no minimum waits, no "at least N seconds".
66	
67	**Why:** the SPA-render tail carried a 45-second FLOOR via `Math.max`, so a few shells always cost 45s or more and the ~45s-any-website capability was lost; the measured fix (E-236) restored a 43s crawl to 6.9s with identical accuracy (digest-claude-ledger-forensic §d).
68	
69	**Enforced by:** `tools/domain-gates/budget-caps.js` (AST scan for floor patterns on budget variables) in `.github/workflows/domain-gates.yml`; the Speed specialist's per-stage latency report in `eval/speed-budget.js` fails CI if any stage's configured budget behaves as a floor on the golden set.
70	
71	### Rule 9: Every external step has a hard deadline
72	Every LLM call, browser step, register lookup and fetch is wrapped in a hard `Promise.race` deadline. A slow dependency degrades the mint; it never hangs it.
73	
74	**Why:** a stuck Chromium held a mint hostage for 752 seconds because the 30s goto timeout did not bound browser launch + networkidle; exhausted free LLM tiers burned 30s × 3 retries × 3 gate attempts waiting for answers that were never coming (digest-claude-ledger-forensic b115, §d E-238).
75	
76	**Enforced by:** `evidence/` and `llm/router.js` expose only deadline-wrapped call primitives (no raw fetch/browser call exports); `tools/domain-gates/deadline-audit.js` in `.github/workflows/domain-gates.yml` fails CI on any external call site not routed through a deadline wrapper; timeout behaviour is exercised by hang fixtures in `eval/calibration-known-bad/`.
77	
78	### Rule 10: Three-state findings, and confident voice must be EARNED
79	Every finding is exactly one of **violation / needs-review / pass** (the axe-core doctrine: anything not assertable with certainty degrades to needs-review, and every false positive is a bug). Voice policy (the punchier hybrid, Aman-decided 2026-07-16): the confident "evidenced on your live site" voice is reserved for register-verified facts and adjudicated findings carrying verbatim quotes; needs-review items render in observation language ("detected X; this may implicate Act §Y, penalty band Z"); a standing not-legal-advice line appears on every audit. No finding is ever phrased as an adjudicated legal conclusion.
80	
81	**Why:** binary verdicts forced the verifier to call the real Equality Act fabricated because it could not say "unverifiable"; low-confidence attachments rendered as hard breaches; invented enforcement filler printed when no curated intel existed (digest-claude-ledger-forensic b100, b59, b96; digest-research-compliance §A axe-core, lesson 15).
82	
83	**Enforced by:** the payload schema (`payload/schema/`) types `finding.state` as the closed three-value enum and ties voice tier to state plus evidence fields (confident-voice strings on a needs-review finding = schema-invalid); `render-proof/truth-pack.spec.js` asserts the not-legal-advice line is present and that no needs-review finding renders breach-voice phrasing; `eval/golden/run.js` locks voice regressions.
84	
85	### Rule 11: The LLM is extraction and selection only; it never authors a fact
86	The LLM may select among retrieved candidate spans, extract from supplied material, and adjudicate proposed findings as a FILTER (it can remove or downgrade, never invent). It never writes a law name, fine, citation, jurisdiction or breach into existence. All LLM output is structured (schema-enforced), logged in full, and every prompt/model change is gated on a golden set built at least 60% from real production failures. Temperature 0 is not determinism: log inputs, outputs and model build for every call.
87	
88	**Why:** commercial RAG-backed legal tools still hallucinate in 1 of 6 or more queries (Stanford RegLab); our own self-learning loop invented "Cookies... Regulations 2020" with three different fabricated URLs and resurrected the repealed Disability Discrimination Act 1995 (digest-research-llm-agents Part A; digest-claude-ledger-forensic b97).
89	
90	**Enforced by:** `llm/gate.js` (ported, proven live: deterministic rubrics, never model self-confidence, three strikes to deterministic fallback) wraps every call; the adjudicator's filter-only contract is tested against a hallucinated-id fixture in `llm/evals/run.js` (`.github/workflows/llm-eval.yml`, blocking on precision and abstain-rate); full call logging asserted by `eval/calibration-known-bad/` replay fixtures.
91	
92	### Rule 12: The five structural-impossibility LLM gates (fail-closed AND-chain)
93	Every LLM-touched claim must pass ALL five gates. They compose: gates 1 and 2 are deterministic set/string operations, so fabricated citations and fake quotes have escape probability zero, not merely small. Everything rejected routes to abstention; the failure mode is "refuses to answer", never "confidently wrong".
94	
95	**Gate 1: Retrieval-gated emission.** A claim may cite only a `source_id` present in the injected retrieval set; a citation to a non-retrieved authority is unrepresentable in the output schema.
96	*Why:* 13-21% of legal citations across five commercial systems were hallucinated (arXiv 2606.00898). *Enforced by:* structured-output schemas in `llm/prompts/` whose source_id fields are constrained to the injected candidate list, validated post-hoc by `llm/gate.js` (out-of-set id = score 0, reject).
97	
98	**Gate 2: Verbatim-quote exact re-match.** Every quoted span must survive a normalised exact-substring match against the cited document before the claim may exist.
99	*Why:* models paraphrase inside quotation marks and fabricate pincites (Stanford HAI); our corpus regex ran on raw HTML while the quote came from stripped text, so hit and quote disagreed (digest-claude-ledger-forensic b32). *Enforced by:* `breach/verifiers/quote-match.js` (deterministic, blocking, one detection surface per rule); re-verified at render by `render-proof/truth-pack.spec.js`.
100	
101	**Gate 3: NLI entailment per claim.** Each atomic claim is the hypothesis, its cited span the premise; anything not labelled entailment is rejected.
102	*Why:* fluent-but-neutral sentences are the classic grounded hallucination (Nature 2024 semantic entropy; Google NLI research). *Enforced by:* the entailment step in `breach/adjudicator/` with its verdict rubric scored in `llm/gate.js`; calibration fixtures with known neutral/contradiction pairs in `llm/evals/` must be rejected every run.
103	
104	**Gate 4: Abstain-by-default confidence floor.** The default output is insufficient-evidence; a claim releases only above a calibrated floor, and abstention is always the cheaper path.
105	*Why:* forcing an answer on every query "causes confident mistakes and hallucinations" (SelectLLM); ambiguous verdicts defaulting to CONFIRMED kept fines on findings (digest-claude-ledger-forensic b135). *Enforced by:* the floor constant lives in `breach/adjudicator/` and is regression-locked by `llm/evals/run.js` abstain-rate gates; lowering it without updating the eval set fails CI.
106	
107	**Gate 5: Diverse jury with veto-to-reject.** P0/P1 findings require a quorum of genuinely independent model families; any single veto rejects; the jury may never veto curated catalogue facts (SECTOR_CORE / SECTOR_AGNOSTIC immunity).
108	*Why:* weak judges have high true-positive but very low true-negative rates, so a rejection is more trustworthy than an approval; our "dual-model" cross-check once hit the same provider twice and was not independent; the LLM was once allowed to veto the SRA off a solicitors' firm (digest-research-llm-agents Pattern 5; digest-claude-ledger-forensic b73, b91). *Enforced by:* `llm/router.js` quorum configuration asserts distinct provider families; immunity sets tested in `llm/evals/run.js`; forked verifier legs share one guarded code path (the b92 lesson) checked by `tools/one-door/check.js`.
109	
110	### Rule 13: Serves is not bound: the Tier A/B/C jurisdiction matrix
111	A jurisdiction attaches only on evidence, graded by tier: **Tier A** (register row, regulatory authorisation, registered office) scores 5; **Tier B** (office postcode, local phone, ccTLD/hreflang, local currency) scores 3; **Tier C** (prose mentions, "we serve clients in...") scores 1. Attachment requires one Tier A or two Tier B signals. Tier C NEVER attaches law; at most it feeds an advisory tier. Serving a market is not being bound by its law; law applicability is then a pure set-membership query over the applicability matrix (jurisdiction × sector × activity tags), and the verdict is graded with an evidence chain, KYB-style, never a boolean.
112	
113	**Why:** `/incorporated in/` anchored to no country judged 6 of 7 real UK/EU/UAE law-firm footers "established in the United States", and Mills & Reeve (UK) was served US ABA rules and ADA law, 4 of 8 findings jurisdictionally void; template hreflang pulled EU law onto a US clinic; a jurisdiction statement told a UAE firm the CCPA applied (digest-findings-bible DG-05; digest-claude-ledger-forensic b55, b128; digest-research-compliance §C).
114	
115	**Enforced by:** `facts/jurisdiction.js` is the single door for jurisdiction (Rule 1 gate applies); `tools/domain-gates/nexus-anchoring.js` in `.github/workflows/domain-gates.yml` replays the known-bad footer corpus (the Mills & Reeve class) every run; `eval/reference-set/run.js` fails on any jurisdiction contradiction against the hand-verified firms.
116	
117	### Rule 14: Provenance-mandatory catalogue rows
118	No catalogue row (law, rule, penalty, enforcement case) exists without provenance: source, official URL verified by FETCHING it and confirming the page IS that law (a 200 with matching content, never a 202 or an unfetched string), and last_synced. Discovered candidates are three-state (verified / rejected / unverifiable); only verified rows are promoted, and promotion of legal judgements is human-gated.
119	
120	**Why:** the official-URL "gate" checked only that a URL string was present; legislation.gov.uk answers 202-with-empty-body to ANY URL shape, so the status-code gate passed every fabrication; a full discovery run over 151 candidates yielded zero promotable laws (digest-claude-ledger-forensic b97, b98, b99).
121	
122	**Enforced by:** `catalogue/lint/provenance.js` and `catalogue/lint/citation-completeness.js` (blocking) in `.github/workflows/catalogue-lint.yml`: every row must carry resolvable provenance fields and every ACTIVE rule a citation; the catalogue compiler in `catalogue/` refuses to emit an artifact containing an unverified row; enforcement-intel population is human-gated per `catalogue/lint/enforcement-crossval.js`.
123	
124	---
125	
126	## Part II: Supplementary mechanical rules (earned by specific failures)
127	
128	### Rule 15: No scan cache; the engine version is load-bearing
129	A legal-evidence engine keeps no scan cache (a day-old scan dated today is not evidence). Any change to scan logic bumps ENGINE_VERSION, the version rides the idempotency key, and the DB-level trigger keyed on `engine_flags.required_engine_version` is the lock every minter must pass.
130	
131	**Why:** `scanner_cache` replayed pre-fix scans so "it's fixed now" was false four rounds running; a rogue pre-gate Hetzner cron minted on v19 for days; a partial catalogue (290 frameworks, 0 rules) was cached process-wide and connect attached nothing (digest-claude-ledger-forensic §c, b44, b107).
132	
133	**Enforced by:** `.github/workflows/engine-version-guard.yml` fails any PR touching scan logic without a version bump; the DB gate is ported as part of `mint/`; `tools/mint-reconciler/reconcile.js` alarms on any row minted under a stale version.
134	
135	### Rule 16: No secrets, ever (public repo)
136	Nothing matching a credential shape (ghp_*, npg_*, sk-*, cfut_*, xoxb-*, postgres://, or any key/token/password) may appear in any file, fixture, log capture or golden payload. Golden fixtures are structural-only and pseudonymised; raw payloads with prospect PII stay gitignored.
137	
138	**Why:** a leaked webhook secret persisted verbatim in a harvested review snapshot on the old estate (digest-findings-bible GR-0002/CR-0010); golden fixtures nearly leaked prospect PII into a public repo (digest-claude-ledger-forensic b122).
139	
140	**Enforced by:** `.github/workflows/secret-scan.yml` (gitleaks, blocking, full history on push) plus GitHub push protection; `eval/golden/` fixture linter rejects email addresses and person names outside the pseudonym dictionary.
141	
142	### Rule 17: Done means verified against ground truth
143	No agent, script or session reports a change done until verified at the end against ground truth: the git diff, the DB row, the live minted payload, the deployed asset. In-loop assumptions ("already present, skip") are not verification.
144	
145	**Why:** a 17-node sector insert was reported done three times while silently skipping; PR #114 on the website was an empty merge whose "deploy success" shipped nothing; a fix merged to one leg while the quorum leg kept the bug (digest-claude-ledger-forensic b117, b118, §c).
146	
147	**Enforced by:** Rule 7's mint reconciler for engine output; `render-proof/truth-pack.spec.js` for rendered output; CI status on the actual merge commit is the only accepted "done" signal per AGENTS.md Fleet Rule 1.
148	
149	---
150	
151	## Part III: The gate map (rule → enforcing file)
152	
153	**Legend:** files LIVE today: `tools/one-door/check.js`, `tools/swallow-gate/check.js`, `tools/fact-lineage/check.js`, `tools/sweep/*`, `eval/calibration-known-bad/run.js`, `eval/golden/run.js`, `eval/reference-set/verify.js`, `payload/contract/index.js`, `payload/schema/payload.schema.json`, and workflows `ci.yml`, `sweep.yml`, `codeql.yml`, `semgrep.yml`. Every other file in this table is **PLANNED (P1+)**: it is the committed name the owning phase must land, and this table is the contract for it. A planned gate is not an excuse — the rule binds from day one; until its dedicated file lands, the closest live gate (sweep/ci) covers what it can and the rest is enforced in review.
154	
155	| Rule | Enforcing gate | File |
156	|---|---|---|
157	| 1 One door per fact | one-door + fact-lineage | `tools/one-door/check.js` (live, runs in ci/sweep), `tools/fact-lineage/check.js` (live, arms fully in P4) |
158	| 2 Catalogue-only law facts | catalogue lint + truth-pack | `catalogue/lint/no-literal-law-facts.js` (planned P2), `render-proof/truth-pack.spec.js` (planned P4); today `tools/one-door/check.js` (live) blocks fine/regulator/law-title literals outside `catalogue/` |
159	| 3 No artifact no breach | payload schema + verifiers | `payload/schema/payload.schema.json` (live), `breach/verifiers/` (planned P3), `.github/workflows/render-truth.yml` (planned P4) |
160	| 4 Fail-closed calibrated gates | known-bad corpus + silent-swallow AST | `eval/calibration-known-bad/run.js` (live, in ci.yml), `tools/swallow-gate/check.js` (live, in ci.yml + sweep) |
161	| 5 Reachable-or-DORMANT | reachability walk | `DORMANT.md` (lands with the first module), reachability walk live in `tools/sweep/collect-local.js` (arms when `mint/worker.js` or `mint/index.js` exists); madge + dependency-cruiser live in ci.yml |
162	| 6 Quarantine below confidence | adjudicator contract + fixtures | `breach/adjudicator/` (planned P3), `eval/calibration-known-bad/run.js` (live) |
163	| 7 Mint = row+200+truth-pack | post-write assertions + reconciler | `mint/post-write-assertions.js`, `tools/mint-reconciler/reconcile.js`, `.github/workflows/mint-reconcile.yml` (all planned P4) |
164	| 8 Budgets are caps | budget-caps gate + speed report | `tools/domain-gates/budget-caps.js`, `eval/speed-budget.js`, `.github/workflows/domain-gates.yml` (all planned P3) |
165	| 9 Hard external deadlines | deadline-audit gate | `tools/domain-gates/deadline-audit.js` (planned P3), hang fixtures in `eval/calibration-known-bad/` |
166	| 10 Three-state + earned voice | payload schema + truth-pack + golden | `payload/schema/payload.schema.json` (live), `render-proof/truth-pack.spec.js` (planned P4), `eval/golden/run.js` (live) |
167	| 11 LLM extraction/selection only | gate rubric + LLM eval harness | `llm/gate.js`, `llm/evals/run.js`, `.github/workflows/llm-eval.yml` (all planned P3) |
168	| 12 The 5 structural gates | schema constraint, quote-match, entailment, floor, quorum | `llm/prompts/` schemas, `breach/verifiers/quote-match.js`, `breach/adjudicator/`, `llm/router.js`, `llm/evals/run.js` (all planned P3) |
169	| 13 Tier A/B/C jurisdiction | nexus-anchoring + reference set | `facts/jurisdiction.js` (planned P2), `tools/domain-gates/nexus-anchoring.js` (planned P3), `eval/reference-set/verify.js` (live) |
170	| 14 Provenance-mandatory catalogue | provenance + citation lints | `catalogue/lint/provenance.js`, `catalogue/lint/citation-completeness.js` (all planned P2) |
171	| 15 No cache + version gate | engine-version guard + DB trigger | `.github/workflows/engine-version-guard.yml` (planned P4), `mint/` DB gate (planned P4) |
172	| 16 No secrets | secret scan + fixture linter | Semgrep p/secrets pack (live, `.github/workflows/semgrep.yml`); `.github/workflows/secret-scan.yml` gitleaks lane + `eval/golden/` fixture linter (planned P1) |
173	| 17 Done = ground truth | reconciler + truth-pack + CI status | `tools/mint-reconciler/reconcile.js`, `render-proof/truth-pack.spec.js` (planned P4); CI status on the merge commit (live) |
174	
175	Also always-on (the 12-tool sweep, per PRD §7): CodeQL (`.github/workflows/codeql.yml`, live), Semgrep (`.github/workflows/semgrep.yml`, live), CodeRabbit and CodeScene (GitHub Apps, least-privilege, cannot write code; founder enables per docs/FOUNDER-ACTIONS.md), madge + dependency-cruiser (live in `.github/workflows/ci.yml`), jscpd and ESLint (live in `.github/workflows/ci.yml`), the sweep orchestrator (`.github/workflows/sweep.yml`, live nightly), the domain gates (`.github/workflows/domain-gates.yml`, planned P3), and the Playwright render lane (`.github/workflows/render-truth.yml`, planned P4). SARIF fan-in, fingerprint dedupe, DSU clustering and the numbered ledger live in `tools/sweep/`. ACT (two or more independent tools agree) blocks all progress until fixed; a single-tool finding is a lead, never auto-fixed.
176

content.content:
1	# catalogue/ - the Compliance Object Model, its gates, and the compiled artifact
2	
3	The catalogue is the ONLY source of law names, citations, fines, penalty bands, regulators and
4	enforcement intel anywhere in this engine (Constitution Rule 2). Nothing in `breach/`, `render-proof/`,
5	or any future renderer holds a law fact of its own; every fact a client sees traces back to exactly
6	one row in this directory's compiled artifact. This file documents the record model, the four gates
7	every row must clear, how a pack graduates into that artifact, and how a shipped row later activates
8	into something the mint can actually attach to a live audit.
9	
10	---
11	
12	## 1. The record model (the Compliance Object Model, "COM")
13	
14	A **pack** is one JSON file under `catalogue/packs/<cell>.json`:
15	
16	```jsonc
17	{
18	  "cell": "uk-legal",          // matches the filename (schema-enforced)
19	  "jurisdiction": "UK",        // a facts/vocabulary.js jurisdiction code
20	  "generated": "2026-07-17",   // YYYY-MM-DD, the pack AUTHORING date (not a build stamp)
21	  "records": [ /* COM record, COM record, ... */ ]
22	}
23	```
24	
25	A **record** is one law/rule/duty. The full required shape is enforced by `catalogue/schema.js`
26	(`validateRecord`/`validatePack` - the one door for record/pack SHAPE, per Constitution Rule 1) and
27	is not repeated field-by-field here; read `schema.js`'s own header for the exact contract and the
28	scope decisions behind it (why `sector` accepts a `'universal'` sentinel, why `sub_sector` is
29	type-checked but not enum-checked, why `sub_jurisdiction` accepts `null`/`'multi'`/a modelled code).
30	In outline, a record carries:
31	
32	- **Identity**: `id` (upper-snake), `name`, `jurisdiction`, `sub_jurisdiction`.
33	- **Attachment surface**: `sector[]`, `sub_sector[]`, `activity_tags[]`, `required_nexus[]`,
34	  `applies_when[]`, `excluded_when[]` (Constitution Rule 13: a jurisdiction/law attaches on
35	  evidence, never on "serves a market").
36	- **The duty**: `website_obligations[]`, each `{duty, elements[], evidence_type}` where
37	  `evidence_type` is one of `presence` / `absence` / `behavioural` / `register` (see §3 below - this
38	  is the field the polarity linter reads).
39	- **Money**: `penalty {typical_low, typical_high, statutory_max, currency, basis, max_is_rare}`
40	  (Constitution Rule 2/14: no code file anywhere else may hold a fine figure).
41	- **Who enforces it**: `regulator {name, register_url}`, `enforcement[]` (real cases, each with
42	  `case, date, amount, url, summary`).
43	- **Why it matters to the client**: `intel {why_matters, regulator_asks_first, relevance_hook}`.
44	- **Provenance** (Constitution Rule 14, mandatory on every row): `provenance {sources[],
45	  seed_status, verified_date}`.
46	- **Lifecycle**: `status` - one of `candidate` / `needs_verification` / `rejected_qa` (see §4).
47	- **Citation**: `citation {act, section, url}` - the URL is checked against an allowlist of genuine
48	  statutory/regulatory/court hosts (`catalogue/linters/citation-completeness.js`'s `OFFICIAL_HOSTS`),
49	  strictly for any `status: "candidate"` row.
50	
51	---
52	
53	## 2. The QA-sidecar rule (a pack is compilable only with a human sign-off)
54	
55	`catalogue/compile.js` reads every `catalogue/packs/*.json` file, but only ever COMPILES a pack that
56	has a same-named `.QA.md` sidecar sitting next to it:
57	
58	```
59	catalogue/packs/uk-legal.json      <- authored pack
60	catalogue/packs/uk-legal.QA.md     <- the human legal-QA sign-off for THAT pack
61	```
62	
63	A pack with no sidecar is **excluded** from compilation with a loud log line
64	(`pack excluded: no legal-QA sidecar - ...`) - not a warning, not a soft partial-include. At the
65	time this compiler was built, `catalogue/packs/uk-tech-media-industrial.json` has no sidecar and is
66	therefore excluded; none of its records can reach the compiled artifact until its own `.QA.md` lands.
67	
68	The sidecar is not a rubber stamp - read any of the six existing ones
69	(`catalogue/packs/*.QA.md`) and you will find the actual method every reviewer used: independent
70	fetches of the highest-penalty and every `gap_filled` citation against primary sources
71	(legislation.gov.uk, ftc.gov, oag.ca.gov, federalregister.gov, regulator sites), a polarity red-team
72	pass, a fines/currency sanity pass, and per-record verdicts (`confirmed` / `corrected` /
73	`downgraded to rejected_qa` / `CRITICAL`). This is the human step Constitution Rule 14 exists to
74	force: "promotion of legal judgements is human-gated." The compiler does not, and cannot, replace it
75	- it only refuses to ship anything that skipped it.
76	
77	---
78	
79	## 3. The linter fleet (what runs on every compilable pack, every time)
80	
81	Four linters, each with its own doctrine documented in its own file header - this section is a map,
82	not a duplicate of that documentation:
83	
84	| Linter | Catches | Blocking rule id(s) | Warning rule id(s) |
85	|---|---|---|---|
86	| `catalogue/linters/citation-completeness.js` | Rule 14/C-104: a `candidate` row's `citation.url` on a non-official host; missing `provenance.sources`; missing `penalty.currency`/`basis`; an `enforcement[]` entry with no `url`/`date` | `citation-missing`, `citation-host-unofficial`, `provenance-sources-empty`, `penalty-currency-missing`, `penalty-basis-missing`, `enforcement-url-missing`, `enforcement-date-missing`, `enforcement-host-unofficial`, `register-host-unofficial` | *(none - every rule id above is emitted at `level: 'error'`; see §6 on why this compiler takes that literally)* |
87	| `catalogue/linters/polarity.js` | C-046/047/048: a duty containing PROHIBITION language typed anything other than `evidence_type: 'absence'`; REQUIREMENT language typed anything other than `presence`/`register` | `polarity-prohibition-mismatch`, `polarity-requirement-mismatch` | `negation-guard-needed` (an `absence` duty whose text also carries self-declaration wording - the Botox-U18 class, C-048/C-060: a naive presence-check risks matching the site's OWN compliant statement) |
88	| `catalogue/linters/regex-health.js` | C-050: a `pattern`/`regex`/`detect`/etc. field that does not compile, has no `positive_example`, or does not match its own `positive_example` (the over-escaped dead-regex class) | `regex-health/pattern-does-not-compile`, `regex-no-positive-example`, `regex-dead-pattern` | *(none)* - and it honestly reports "0 patterns" when a pack carries none, which is the current, correct state for every COM pack today (duties are prose, not regex, until a future detection migration) |
89	| `catalogue/linters/threshold-guard.js` | C-071: a size/turnover-threshold-bearing record with an empty `excluded_when` (the Modern Slavery Act-on-an-SME class); C-096: a `statutory_max` with no modelled typical band | `threshold-excluded-when-missing` | `typical-band-missing` |
90	
91	### Polarity semantics, restated (read `polarity.js`'s own header for the full doctrine)
92	
93	`evidence_type` describes **what the check looks for on the client's site**, not what the law
94	requires in the abstract: `presence` fires when required content is MISSING; `absence` fires when
95	prohibited content IS PRESENT; `register` is a presence-family check against a register row rather
96	than page text; `behavioural` is exempt from polarity-language checks entirely, because an observed
97	action can legitimately be phrased either way depending on what actually fired the check. Getting
98	this backwards is exactly the DG-02 defect (C-048): a `must_appear` rule that fired on the ABSENCE
99	of a boastful phrase, breaching firms for things they did NOT say.
100	
101	### `catalogue/linters/lib.js`
102	
103	Not a linter itself - the shared pack/fixture loader every linter above calls (`loadRecords`,
104	`resolveJsonFiles`, `hostMatchesAllowlist`). One door for "how a linter finds its input and what
105	shape a record is in", so four linters do not grow four slightly different loaders.
106	
107	---
108	
109	## 4. Record lifecycle: `status`
110	
111	| `status` | Meaning | Ships in the compiled artifact? |
112	|---|---|---|
113	| `candidate` | Authored, passed the pack's own `.QA.md` legal-QA pass, passed every linter | **Yes** |
114	| `needs_verification` | A specific fact inside the record could not be confirmed at authoring time | **No** - stays in the source pack (visible, logged), excluded from `catalogue.v1.json` |
115	| `rejected_qa` | The pack's own QA reviewer downgraded this record (unverifiable, wrong statute, or worse) | **No** - stays in the source pack, excluded |
116	
117	`catalogue/compile.js` enforces the "No / No" column: a record is filtered OUT of `records[]` at
118	assembly time if its `status` is `needs_verification` or `rejected_qa`, and the compiler logs every
119	such exclusion by id, cell and status so nothing disappears silently. It is NOT deleted from the
120	pack file - the record stays exactly where its author and QA reviewer put it, in case a later pass
121	resolves the open question and promotes it.
122	
123	94 of the 95 records across the six QA'd packs are `candidate`; one, `US_STATE_PRIVACY_WAVE_2025_26`
124	(us-universal), is `needs_verification` - an aggregate multi-state overview cannot carry one
125	official citation, so it is excluded from the artifact until a future pass splits it into per-state
126	records each with its own official citation (see the record's own `qa_note`). None is `rejected_qa`.
127	The exclusion path exists, is exercised by this one real record, and is tested
128	(`catalogue/compile.test.js`).
129	
130	**`candidate` is not "active."** Shipping in `catalogue.v1.json` means a row is available to be
131	read by the P3+ attachment/breach pipeline once it exists; it is P3+'s own human-sign-off gate
132	(outside this compiler's scope) that promotes a compiled `candidate` row into something the mint
133	actually attaches to a live audit. This compiler's job stops at "compiled, gated, deterministic
134	artifact" - it activates nothing.
135	
136	---
137	
138	## 5. How a pack graduates (the whole path, start to artifact)
139	
140	```
141	  author writes catalogue/packs/<cell>.json
142	              |
143	              v
144	  a human runs an independent legal-QA pass and writes catalogue/packs/<cell>.QA.md
145	  (fetches primary sources, red-teams polarity, sanity-checks fines, downgrades what fails)
146	              |
147	              v
148	  catalogue/compile.js discovers the pack: sidecar present -> COMPILABLE
149	  (no sidecar -> EXCLUDED, loud log line, stops here)
150	              |
151	              v
152	  catalogue/schema.js validatePack() - shape gate (Rule 1)
153	              |
154	              v
155	  the linter fleet (citation-completeness, polarity, regex-health, threshold-guard) - content gate
156	              |
157	              v
158	  ANY error-severity finding -> compilation REFUSED (exit 1). No artifact written.
159	  This compiler never edits a pack or waters down a linter to force a pass (see catalogue/README.md
160	  and the compiler's own header) - a real finding on real content is reported verbatim and the run
161	  stops so a human can fix the SOURCE (the pack, or its QA sidecar).
162	              |
163	              v   (zero errors)
164	  assemble: drop rejected_qa/needs_verification records, sort deterministically, hash
165	              |
166	              v
167	  catalogue/dist/catalogue.v1.json written (gitignored - always regenerated, never committed)
168	```
169	
170	---
171	
172	## 6. What a real finding means (do not water this down)
173	
174	If `node catalogue/compile.js --stamp <...>` reports an error-severity finding against a REAL,
175	already-QA'd pack, that is not a bug in the compiler to route around - it is either:
176	
177	1. A defect the pack's own `.QA.md` pass did not catch (fix the pack, get it re-QA'd), or
178	2. A defect in the `.QA.md` sign-off itself (the sidecar claims a clean pass over content a linter
179	   can now catch that it could not before), or
180	3. A linter being stricter than the sidecar anticipated (the sidecar predates this linter fleet
181	   landing) - in which case the right fix is still to correct the SOURCE pack, never to loosen the
182	   linter or special-case a rule id inside this compiler.
183	
184	At the time this compiler was built, running it against the real packs surfaced exactly this class
185	of finding on four records in `us-healthcare.json`/`us-universal.json` - unofficial-host
186	`enforcement[].url`/`regulator.register_url` entries, an uncitable aggregate `citation.url`, and one
187	`sub_sector` value using an underscore instead of the required hyphen-slug format - reported
188	verbatim by `compile.js`, exactly as designed. A subsequent P2 catalogue-hygiene pass fixed the
189	SOURCE, never the gate: the two unofficial enforcement entries were removed (their URLs preserved in
190	`provenance.sources`), the unofficial `register_url` was set to `null`, the uncitable aggregate
191	record was downgraded to `needs_verification` (with a `qa_note` explaining why), and the `sub_sector`
192	slug was corrected. The real packs now compile with zero error-severity findings (two
193	`typical-band-missing` warnings remain, which is the honest, expected state for a bare statutory
194	ceiling with no gathered typical-enforcement band). This history stays here as a worked example of
195	what a real finding means and how it is properly resolved - fix the pack, never the gate.
196	
197	---
198	
199	## 7. Compile determinism
200	
201	`node catalogue/compile.js --stamp <ISO8601> [--out <path>]`:
202	
203	- `--stamp` is REQUIRED and must match `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS(.sss)Z`. There is no `Date.now()`
204	  fallback anywhere in this file - an artifact's `generated` field must be exactly reproducible from
205	  the same pack inputs and the same stamp, forever (the same doctrine as Constitution Rule 15
206	  applied to a build artifact rather than a live scan).
207	- `cells[]` and `records[]` are sorted deterministically (`cell` name, `id` respectively) so two
208	  runs over identical inputs produce byte-identical JSON.
209	- `content_hash` is `sha256` of the artifact's own canonical JSON (recursively key-sorted) with the
210	  `content_hash` field itself excluded from what gets hashed - hashing the hash's own container
211	  would make the hash unreproducible by definition, so `content_hash` is computed over
212	  `{catalogue_version, generated, cells, counts, records}` and then attached.
213	- `catalogue/dist/` is gitignored: the artifact is always a build output, never a committed file.
214	
215	Output shape (`catalogue/dist/catalogue.v1.json`):
216	
217	```jsonc
218	{
219	  "catalogue_version": "v1.0.0-p2",
220	  "generated": "2026-01-01T00:00:00Z",   // the --stamp value, verbatim
221	  "content_hash": "<sha256 hex>",
222	  "cells": [
223	    { "cell": "uk-legal", "jurisdiction": "UK", "pack_generated": "2026-07-17",
224	      "source": "catalogue/packs/uk-legal.json",
225	      "records_total": 12, "records_included": 12, "records_excluded": 0 }
226	    // ...
227	  ],
228	  "counts": {
229	    "packs_scanned": 7, "packs_compilable": 6, "packs_excluded": 1,
230	    "packs_excluded_detail": [ { "cell": "uk-tech-media-industrial", "source": "...", "reason": "no legal-QA sidecar" } ],
231	    "records_scanned": 95, "records_included": 94, "records_excluded": 1,
232	    "records_excluded_by_status": { "needs_verification": 1 }
233	  },
234	  "records": [ /* every included COM record, plus a "cell" field naming its source pack */ ]
235	}
236	```
237	
238	---
239	
240	## 8. Files in this directory
241	
242	| File | Role |
243	|---|---|
244	| `schema.js` (+`.test.js`) | The Compliance Object Model shape validator - `validateRecord`/`validatePack`. One door for record/pack shape. |
245	| `linters/lib.js` | Shared pack/fixture loader for the linter fleet. |
246	| `linters/citation-completeness.js` (+`.test.js`) | Rule 14/C-104 provenance + official-host citation gate. |
247	| `linters/polarity.js` (+`.test.js`) | C-046/047/048 polarity doctrine + negation-guard warning. |
248	| `linters/regex-health.js` (+`.test.js`) | C-050 earn-your-zero regex gate. |
249	| `linters/threshold-guard.js` (+`.test.js`) | C-071 threshold/`excluded_when` gate + C-096 typical-band warning. |
250	| `compile.js` (+`.test.js`) | The compiler documented in this README: packs -> gated -> `catalogue/dist/catalogue.v1.json`. |
251	| `packs/*.json` + `*.QA.md` | The authored packs and their human legal-QA sign-offs. |
252	| `dist/` | Compiler OUTPUT. Gitignored. Never hand-edited, never committed. |
253	
254	Related, outside this directory: `facts/vocabulary.js` (the enum door every schema/linter check
255	delegates to - `jurisdiction`, `sector`, `activity_tags`, `required_nexus` all validate through it,
256	never a parallel list in `catalogue/`), `eval/calibration-known-bad/run.js` (wires
257	`catalogue-citation-completeness`, `catalogue-polarity`, `catalogue-regex-health` and
258	`catalogue-threshold-guard` into the repo-wide earn-your-zero gate against the `p2-*.json` fixtures
259	in `eval/calibration-known-bad/fixtures/`), `facts/served-cells.json` (the ICP gate dataset -
260	separate from, and downstream of, which catalogue cells exist: a cell can be compiled here and still
261	not be `served: true` there until the ICP gate is updated).

1	# facts/ — the one-door fact layer
2	
3	Every client-facing fact has exactly ONE producer here (Constitution Rule 1). A consumer reads
4	the fact; it never re-derives it. Each module is a **pure, synchronous function over an
5	`EvidenceBundle`**: it never touches the network, a clock, or the environment, and it never
6	guesses. Register rows arrive pre-fetched on the bundle; page text arrives pre-stripped. The
7	producers are `identity.js`, `jurisdiction.js`, `sector.js`, `capabilities.js`, and the shared
8	token door `vocabulary.js`.
9	
10	## The EvidenceBundle (input)
11	
12	```
13	{
14	  domain: 'example.co.uk',
15	  corpus: {
16	    pages: [{ url, title, text, jsonLd: [...], ogSiteName? }],   // text = stripped visible text
17	    footerText?: '...'                                            // statutory disclosure block
18	  },
19	  registers: { companiesHouse?, gleif?, sra?, cqc?, fca?, ico? }  // pre-fetched rows, may be {}
20	}
21	```
22	
23	`text` is stripped visible text only (caution C-012): no `<script>`/`<style>`, and sector scoring
24	never reads `title`, `ogSiteName` or `jsonLd` (those are identity signals). An unreachable bundle
25	(bot-walled or an unrendered SPA shell) carries no readable corpus; the tolerant doors abstain
26	across the board on it rather than inventing.
27	
28	## The confidence ladder (every fact grades on it)
29	
30	`register > corroborated > weak > abstain` (`vocabulary.CONFIDENCE_LEVELS`).
31	
32	- **register** — a register row (or an authorisation statement with a number) backs the fact.
33	- **corroborated** — two or more independent on-page sources agree (or an on-site Tier-A signal).
34	- **weak** — a single unrivalled source; attaches, but never more than weakly.
35	- **abstain** — nothing safe. A `null`/omitted fact is a first-class, correct outcome.
36	
37	**Doctrine: match or abstain, never contradict.** A missing field is fine; a wrong one ends the
38	company. Ambiguity always defaults to withholding, never to a default value. `eval/reference-set/
39	run-facts.js` enforces this against hand-verified firms: MATCH and ABSTAIN pass, CONTRADICT fails.
40	
41	## vocabulary.js — the shared token door
42	
43	The ONE producer of the non-legal token vocabularies the facts layer reads: the canonical sector
44	`TREE`/`SECTORS` (+ `SECTOR_ALIASES`, `CANONICAL_SECTORS`, `SUB_EXCLUSIVE`, `DOMAIN_SELF_IDENTITY`),
45	the jurisdiction sets (`JURISDICTIONS`, `SUB_JURISDICTIONS`, `COUNTRY_TOKENS`, `FAMILY_ALIAS`,
46	`AE_FEDERAL_DP_TOKEN`), `NEXUS_TYPES`, `ACTIVITY_TAGS`, and the grading enums (`CONFIDENCE_LEVELS`,
47	`FINDING_STATES`). Validators: `canonicalSector`, `famCanon`, `isCanonicalSector`, `isJurisdiction`,
48	`isActivityTag`, `sectorSelfIdFromDomain`, and `assertVocab(kind, value)` which **fails closed**
49	(throws on an unknown kind or value; Rule 4). Everything exported is deep-frozen. It holds **NO**
50	law names, citations, fines or regulator strings (Rule 2); those are catalogue-owned. Every detect
51	regex is word-boundary anchored on every alternation (C-059) and ships a known-positive sample
52	proven by `vocabulary.test.js` (the C-050 dead-regex guard).
53	
54	## identity.js — `resolveIdentity(bundle)`
55	
56	Sole producer of `display_name`, `legal_name`, `company_number`, `registered_office`, `slug`.
57	Returns `{ fact:'identity', domain, display_name, legal_name, company_number, registered_office,
58	slug, rejected, notes, vocabulary_source }` where each fact field is an envelope
59	`{ value, confidence, evidence[] }`. The ladder, highest first: (1) Companies House row corroborated
60	by an on-page identifier, (2) schema.org Organization/LegalService name, (3) `ogSiteName`,
61	(4) footer identity block, (5) `<title>` split on the site separator with marketing tails removed,
62	(6) domain stem (always-clean last resort, `weak`). Rejected candidates fall through and never
63	poison the result: generic page furniture, headlines over 6 words, HTML-entity residue (the
64	`amp`/`x27` slug class, C-003), and strings sharing no token with the domain unless
65	register-corroborated (C-002). The slug derives ONLY from the resolved name, never a page title.
66	
67	## jurisdiction.js — `resolveJurisdiction(bundle)`
68	
69	Sole producer of the nexus. Returns `{ bound:[{ jurisdiction, tier_evidence[], confidence, score }],
70	serves[], sub_jurisdictions[], abstained }`. `serves[]` (marketing reach) is separate from `bound[]`
71	(legal nexus): no consumer may attach law from `serves` (C-008). The **Tier A/B/C matrix**
72	(`TIER_WEIGHTS = {A:5, B:3, C:1}`, Rule 13):
73	
74	- **Tier A (5, each alone binds)** — a register row with a real identifier; an on-site authorisation
75	  statement naming an authority WITH a number; a registered-office / incorporated-in statement
76	  anchored to a named country in the same span.
77	- **Tier B (3)** — country-format postcode, local phone country code, ccTLD, pricing currency.
78	  Binding needs **two independent Tier-B kinds**, never two of the same kind (C-007).
79	- **Tier C (1)** — marketing prose, bar admissions, bare authority mentions. **Never binds**; feeds
80	  `serves[]` only.
81	
82	Nexus is anchored (C-009, the Mills & Reeve ghost-US class): "incorporated in" binds only to a
83	country named inside the establishment span. Sub-jurisdictions: US states on observable state nexus,
84	UK nation from the postcode, and DIFC/ADGM free-zone establishment as a distinct typed nexus that
85	**displaces** the AE federal DP regime (DIFC takes precedence; "DIFC Courts" advocacy never
86	establishes). Empty `bound[]` means `abstained: true`; there is no default jurisdiction (C-006).
87	
88	## sector.js — `resolveSector(bundle, options)` + the ICP gate
89	
90	Returns `{ fact:'sector', value:{ sector, sub_sector }|null, confidence, evidence[],
91	contradictions[], diagnostics }`. Deterministic doctrine:
92	
93	- **Two-cue deny-by-default (E-005):** a sector attaches only when >= 2 distinct visible-text cues
94	  beat every rival from a different family. A weak or tied score selects nothing; "General" does not
95	  exist.
96	- **Own-identity guard:** client-industry mentions ("law firm SEO", "we help law firms") are
97	  discounted, so a marketing agency never classifies as its clients.
98	- **Domain self-identity (C-013):** the firm's own domain naming what it IS
99	  (`sectorSelfIdFromDomain`) can win the two-cue MARGIN over a rival that appears only as incidental
100	  body mentions — but never lowers the floor, so a self-identifying domain with < 2 body cues still
101	  resolves nothing (the immigrationlawyersusa class, caution C-006).
102	- **Register cross-check (C-014/C-016):** an SRA/CQC/FCA row or a Companies House SIC family decides
103	  or corroborates the family; a register or SIC that CONTRADICTS the text family downgrades to
104	  abstain. A contradicted sector never ships.
105	- A queue **hint** is never evidence: it never resolves or overrides a sector, only flags a
106	  disagreement for upstream re-verification.
107	
108	`resolveSectorWithLlm(bundle, options)` is an async seam, never called by default: it runs only when
109	the deterministic path abstained WITHOUT a register contradiction, may select only a sector already
110	in the tree (closed world, Rule 11), is capped at `weak`, and fails closed to the abstention on any
111	hook error (recorded as a typed degradation, never swallowed).
112	
113	**The ICP gate — `auditableCell({ sector, sub_sector, jurisdictions_bound })`.** The only reader of
114	`served-cells.json`. Returns `{ auditable, reason, cell? }`. A `(sector, sub_sector, jurisdiction)`
115	triple is auditable ONLY when it matches a `served:true` cell; anything else is refused with a
116	stated reason ("sector unresolved", "no bound jurisdiction", "cell not served yet; activates: …",
117	"not in the served-cells manifest"). The manifest is UK-depth-first: every UK sector cell is served
118	(`sub_sectors:'*'`); EU/US/AE/SA/QA are `served:false` with an `activates` note (the P5 waves).
119	Silence is free: the engine refuses an out-of-dataset cell rather than guessing.
120	
121	## capabilities.js — `deriveCapabilities(bundle)`
122	
123	Sole producer of the 14 activity predicates (`CAPABILITY_TAGS`: `b2c`, `b2b_only`, `ecommerce`,
124	`cookies_present`, `runs_ads`, `uses_ai`, `payments`, `ugc`, `biometrics`, `child_directed`,
125	`health_claims`, `financial_promotion`, `sells_food_online`, `sells_travel_packages`) that gate
126	capability-scoped law. Returns `{ fact:'CAPABILITIES', predicates:{ <tag>:{ tag, present:
127	true|false|'unknown', confidence, evidence[] } }, exclusions:{ smb_likely }, meta }`. **UNKNOWN is
128	the honest default.** `present:true` requires a verbatim-quotable on-site signal (an exact substring
129	of the scanned text, so it survives a Gate-2 re-match); `present:false` is itself a claim, asserted
130	only where the corpus is deep enough (`MIN_PAGES_FOR_ABSENCE`) and a positive counter-signal exists.
131	A register row never flips a content predicate true on its own (C-060). Tags are validated against
132	`vocabulary.ACTIVITY_TAGS`; drift fails closed at load (`VOCABULARY_LINKED`).
133	
134	## How P3 populates the bundle
135	
136	The facts layer is pure and already stable; P3 fills the `evidence/` collectors that build the
137	bundle it consumes: `evidence/crawler/` produces `corpus.pages` as stripped visible text (E-236
138	parallelism, coverage-contract); `evidence/browser/` adds the rendered DOM and network events (the
139	PECR pre-consent diff); `evidence/registers/` supplies the binary `registers.*` rows (Companies
140	House, GLEIF, SRA, CQC, FCA, ICO) with real name matches (C-004); `evidence/documents/` extracts
141	elements from PDFs. Non-English corpora are gated to `compliance_unassessed` before any fact runs
142	(C-022). The facts modules never fetch: give them a richer bundle and the same pure doors produce a
143	richer, still-abstaining-when-unsure fact set.
144	
145	*Not legal advice. This describes the engine's fact layer, not the law.*
146

105	## 3. Applicability
106	
107	**C-046** The DB stored `rule_type='prohibited'` while the engine tested `=== 'prohibit'`, inverting every prohibition (clean firms flagged, banned-content firms passed) -> code matches the exact enum the DB stores; an eval drives ruleCheck with prohibit+present→breach and prohibit+absent→pass fixtures.
108	
109	**C-047** Botox/U18, FTC fake-reviews, HMR and tenant-fees prohibitions leaked onto any clinic or firm in the sector -> prohibited-content rules require a real subject trigger match, never sector membership alone.
110	
111	**C-048** A `must_appear` ASA rule on "no.1/best" fired when the phrase was ABSENT, and DG-02 found 5 rules breaching firms for NOT advertising what the regulator prohibits (MED_CLAIMS fired on any page containing "clinic") -> a rule-polarity linter proves every prohibition is typed `prohibit` and calibrates each against a boastful fixture (must fail) and a compliant fixture (must pass).
112	
113	**C-049** 240 `trigger_then_check` rules had a trigger but no check regex, returning `unknown` and going mute (~36% of the catalogue) -> the catalogue linter fails any active rule that can neither produce a finding nor is explicitly marked attachment-only.
114	
115	**C-050** 27 stored trigger patterns had corrupted escapes (`\b`→`b`, `\s`→`s`, so `b(bank...)` never matched "bank"), and DG-01 found dead-in-production P0 regexes (`dpo[@\s]`, `vat\s*(no)`, `\bPOM\b`, `FRN ?\d{6}`) that compiled, ran and matched nothing forever -> the regex-health linter proves every stored pattern matches its own intended positive token and rejects over-escaped forms; blocking CI.
116	
117	**C-051** The engine canonicalised the firm's sector but compared against raw `sector_relevance` tags, silently dropping rules tagged `legal`/`wealth`/`health` -> canonicalise both sides of every tag comparison at the gate; a test feeds raw-tagged rules through.
118	
119	**C-052** Barristers were bridged to `law-firms` so chambers inherited SRA solicitor rules; the bug lived in three files and was "fixed" while a copy remained -> node-exclusive frameworks (SRA vs BSB) bind their exact node only; and no mapping fact may have more than one producer file (one-door gate).
120	
121	**C-053** FR_CNIL/DE_BDSG stored `jurisdiction='EU'` so national law leaked onto every EU member firm -> member-state law carries its ISO code; the attachment gate excludes other member states; a linter rejects national statutes tagged with a supranational code.
122	
123	**C-054** National law also leaked via bare-token triggers (`|FR|`, `.de domain`) on unrelated prose -> national law gates on jurisdiction membership evidence, never on leaky token alternations.
124	
125	**C-055** CCPA/CPRA/VCDPA/TDPSA attached to every US firm with no residency/volume nexus; separately, 134 US state rules could never attach because their consumer module was dormant -> threshold/residency-gated laws stay held until detection proves the nexus, and are rendered in an advisory tier when nexus is unprovable; never silently unreachable, never blanket-attached.
126	
127	**C-056** CCPA and CPRA both attached although CPRA superseded CCPA -> the catalogue models supersession; the linter rejects attaching a superseded twin alongside its successor.
128	
129	**C-057** `UK_COMPANIES_ACT` capability gate fired on the adjective "limited" ("limited availability") -> entity gates anchor to company-name/number context, never bare words.
130	
131	**C-058** Consumer-law gates fired on "book a consultation"/"our customers", re-attaching consumer law to the B2B firms it meant to exclude (also RPP, a B2B fintech, got consumer law) -> consumer law requires B2C transaction evidence (pricing, cart, consumer signals), not ubiquitous business language.
132	
133	**C-059** `signalSatisfiesTrigger` substring-matched "post"→postcode and "pay"→payroll -> trigger tokens are word-boundary anchored; substring matching against a rule pattern string is banned by lint.
134	
135	**C-060** `ugc:true` alone attached FTC fake-reviews with no reviews present -> a structured signal never satisfies a content trigger without a real subject match in the corpus.
136	
137	**C-061** UAE_DHA attached to health firms in every emirate, and DHA+DOH+MOHAP all attached to one Abu Dhabi clinic -> emirate-scoped capability gates with per-emirate health-authority exclusivity; attaching two emirate authorities to one establishment fails the linter.
138	
139	**C-062** SAUDI_PDPL/QATAR_PDPPL failed to attach to Saudi/Qatari firms because a redundant capability gate was demanded -> jurisdiction membership IS the nexus for a home data-protection law; no extra gate required.
140	
141	**C-063** The nexus family map covered only UK/EU/US/AE, so 8 routed jurisdictions' nexus gate was a silent no-op -> the family map must cover every routed jurisdiction or the gate fails closed; a completeness test enumerates them.
142	
143	**C-064** DIFC/ADGM could not be distinguished from onshore AE, and free-zone law stacked with UAE_PDPL -> free-zone establishment is a distinct typed nexus; DIFC_DPL/ADGM_DPR displace UAE_PDPL, never stack.
144	
145	**C-065** Citation-presence pseudo-rules ("Section 3 personal data definition disclosure"), seeded to satisfy a text-uniqueness QA gate, masqueraded as duties -> rules are optimised for legal correctness only; citation-presence non-duties are quarantined by the linter.
146	
147	**C-066** Trade bodies (UK_ABI, RICS, Portman) rendered as binding statute, and GOOGLE_EEAT/UK_NMC were mislabelled `statute` -> every framework declares binding_status (statute / statutory_code / professional_code / voluntary_code); the linter cross-checks peer regulators for consistency.
148	
149	**C-067** `specificityRank` treated empty `sector_relevance:[]` as universal, so a sector law lost a lex-specialis tie -> "empty relevance (data gap)" and "genuinely universal" are distinct states in the schema.
150	
151	**C-068** The framework list was built only from findings, so a law that bound but was clean never rendered, and `REG_CAP=10` dropped clean binding laws (PECR vanished) -> every law in the binding map back-fills into the render set; caps govern discretionary rows only; a test asserts a clean-but-binding law renders.
152	
153	**C-069** `fwSectorOK()` silently removed sector-agnostic laws (EC Directive Regs 2002, Legal Services Act 2007) from every audit because they lacked a baseline entry -> an invariant asserts every baseline/universal law is in the universal set; a sector-agnostic law without an explicit baseline entry fails the linter.
154	
155	**C-070** `US_CDC_ART_REPORTING` hit dermatology via the bare tokens "art"/"clinic", and a `US_STATE_PRIVACY` catch-all double-covered named state acts -> tokens are scoped to the sub-sector; catch-alls are deactivated where named acts cover the ground.
156	
157	**C-071** Modern Slavery Act applied to sub-£36m SMEs in 5 of the 9 forensic audits -> `excluded_when` thresholds (turnover, size) are first-class schema fields the gate enforces; the SME fixture is in the calibration corpus.
158	
159	**C-072** Companies Act s.82 fired on a sole trader and on firms whose number was visibly in the footer -> the rule requires an Ltd/LLP signal AND absence of a disclosed number after footer/PDF scanning.
160	
161	**C-073** PECR and "ICO Cookies Guidance" were counted as two breached regimes for one statute -> frameworks mapping to one statute dedupe before counting and before exposure.
162	
163	**C-074** The wrong instrument within the right regulator was attached (FCA MAR to a fin-promo matter; DfE Prevent to a publication-duty matter) -> the catalogue maps sector to the correct sub-instrument; an instrument-level fixture per priority regulator is in the golden set.
164	
165	**C-075** Correctly-tagged firms received only generic web-law because sector packs were missing (estate agency lost the entire property pack and got an F while displaying TPO/ARLA/CMP compliance) -> every priority sub-sector cell either has its pack or the audit discloses the coverage gap; grade credit is given for displayed redress/licence/AML badges.
166	
167	**C-076** When two laws conflicted the engine attached both because the lex-specialis tie-break module was dormant -> conflict precedence is a reviewed legal decision wired into the attachment path, with the review recorded; attaching both sides of a modelled conflict fails the validator.
168	
169	**C-077** The rigorous attachment engine (`resolveLaws`) was an orphan called only by QA while the live path used a weaker `connect()` -> there is exactly one attachment authority and both the mint path and QA call it; the reachability gate proves it.
170	
171	---
172	
173	## 4. Breach & Adjudication
174	
175	**C-078** A bare-word regex over raw HTML accused a real criminal-defence firm of being HACKED ("sex discrimination", `<slot>`, "pornography offences") -> site-compromise detection keys on injected outbound anchor links (off-site, spam-brand, cluster ≥3), never body prose; the mills-reeve-class fixture is in calibration.
176	
177	**C-079** Every breach ever sent was an unreviewed regex match: the LLM gate verified WHICH laws attach, never WHETHER they were broken -> the pipeline is propose (regex/DOM/register) -> verify (deterministic artifact) -> adjudicate (LLM); only adjudicated breaches ship.
178	
179	**C-080** No deterministic artifact stood behind findings -> no artifact, no breach: every shipped breach carries a verbatim quote string-matched to the corpus, a captured network event, a register row, or a failing DOM node; the payload validator enforces the artifact field.
180	
181	**C-081** The adjudicator's verdict died at the findings→pointers whitelist seam (0 pointers carried `adjudicated`) -> every verdict field is asserted to survive end-to-end from adjudicator to payload to rendered DOM; a field-survival test walks each seam.
182	
183	**C-082** Uncertain items shipped as hard breaches -> three output states only (violation / needs-review / pass); nothing uncertain ever ships labelled breach, and needs-review renders in observation language with fines withheld.
184	
185	**C-083** Unadjudicated high-risk findings could reach clients when every LLM provider was down -> any unadjudicated P0 or prohibit finding is demoted to needs-review; the adjudicator is filter-only (remove/downgrade, never invent), tested against a hallucinated-id fixture.
186	
187	**C-084** The adjudicator dropped a real observed PECR breach because the network log was not in the text it saw -> browser-observed and register-observed facts bypass text adjudication; `observed_fact` is a distinct evidence kind.
188	
189	**C-085** `_kindOf()` mapped every compliance finding to `absence`, so browser observations were structurally incapable of confirming and every audit shipped with zero compliance findings (birketts went 0→18 on the fix) -> evidence kind is a declared enum (document-absence, document-presence, observed-behaviour, register-fact) and confirmation logic branches on it; a fixture per kind is in the golden set.
190	
191	**C-086** An ambiguous LLM entailment verdict ("Unclear, leaning no") matched neither branch and the finding stayed CONFIRMED with its fine -> unparseable or ambiguous verdicts default to withholding the accusation, never to keeping it.
192	
193	**C-087** A P0 "your site is hacked" shipped with no citation and no page reference -> every P0 accusation carries locating evidence (URL + quote/artifact) and a citation; the citation gate blocks the mint otherwise.
194	
195	**C-088** An SEO/GEO pointer factory produced findings with no `citation_url` on a legal-flavoured audit -> every finding carries a citation; citation-less breach cards are suppressed at the validator, not styled around.
196
